body_txt: When characters breach internal fourth walls to go on adventures in books, films, and so on. Generally they travel into one or more books or films to become part of the plot, or, at least, to observe the plot firsthand. This often forms part of an allegory or metaphor for escapism, the idea that the imagination allows a reader to 'enter' a work and subconsciously cast themselves as an observer or a main character. This is one reason why the lead characters of books are often very vaguely or loosely described , allowing the reader to assume the hero's identity as a form of role-play. Compare and contrast Trapped in TV Land and The Game Come to Life . See also "Reading Is Cool" Aesop and Portal Book . May include Public Domain Canon Welding . Not to be confused with From Beyond the Fourth Wall , when the fourth wall is the one between us and them.
